search_queries_used: site:endocrine.org testosterone therapy hypogonadism guideline repeat morning result symptoms monitoring 2018
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic hypogonadism diagnosis from one result
automatic testosterone prescription, dose or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Diagnosis requires compatible symptoms and unequivocally, consistently low reliable measurements.
No therapy, dose or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adult men with compatible symptoms and testosterone results; women, children, fertility treatment and gender-affirming care require different guidance.
setting_applicability: Clinician review of previously ordered testosterone results.
UAE_applicability: International specialty guidance requires UAE laboratory, controlled-prescribing, fertility, prostate, cardiovascular and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official Endocrine Society 2018 guideline summary and exact recommendations were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2018 guideline remains current for the Endocrine Society.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact diagnostic section supports result source and date, compatible symptom and negative context, focused examination and clinician-reviewed hormone results without diagnosing hypogonadism.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting compatible symptoms, consistently low morning testosterone context, reliable assay or reference-range context and clinician-selected LH, FSH or further evaluation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact individualized risk-discussion section supports patient goals, measured weight, clinician plan and questions when documented; it does not select testosterone therapy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting fertility plans, comorbidity and contraindication context and shared benefit-risk discussion; no therapy is selected.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact monitoring section supports prior specialist correspondence, safety-net documentation and arranged follow-up only when treatment or review is clinician-led.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of response, adverse effects, adherence and monitoring after treatment initiation without generating thresholds, doses or referral.

unresolved_source_gaps: Sex, age, symptoms, sampling time, fasting, repeat value, assay, reference range, LH/FSH, fertility, comorbidity, medicines, interpretation and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
